skill.name: response-letter
skill.description: Helps organize reviewer comments and generate a standardized Word (.docx) response letter that maps each change to its exact location (page/paragraph/line). Use when revising a manuscript, replying to peer-review feedback, or preparing internal review responses.
skill.summary: Drafts structured peer-review response letters in Word (.docx) format with precise change location tracking across multiple reviewers and revision rounds.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 15 static analysis findings are false positives. The 6 backtick detections are markdown code formatting (inline code with single backticks and code fences with triple backticks), not Ruby or shell command execution. The 5 system reconnaissance and 3 weak cryptographic algorithm detections match patterns in JSON evaluation field names and markdown description text, not executable code. The 2 hardcoded URL detections point to the same legitimate source attribution URL for the skill's GitHub repository. This skill is a pure markdown instruction file with no executable code, network calls, or data exfiltration mechanisms.

low_findings.title: Markdown backticks misidentified as shell command execution
low_findings.description: Six instances of markdown code formatting backticks were flagged as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All are inline code formatting (single backticks around .docx, references/guide.md, and R{reviewer}-{index}) or code fence markers (triple backticks for example usage block). These are standard markdown syntax, not shell execution operators.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL is legitimate source attribution
low_findings.description: The URL https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills on line 7 is the skill's own source repository listed for attribution. This is standard practice for open-source skills and does not indicate unauthorized network activity or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance pattern in evaluation JSON field names
low_findings.description: Four detections in response-letter_audit_result_v1.json at lines 23, 120, 155, and 225 match field names like 'scientific_integrity' and evaluation criteria in a JSON audit results file. These are field names in a structured evaluation report, not system reconnaissance commands.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm pattern in markdown description text
low_findings.description: Three detections at response-letter_audit_result_v1.json:62 and SKILL.md:3,85 match the string pattern 'response' in field names and YAML front matter. These are not cryptographic algorithm references. The letter 'response' in 'response-letter' is unrelated to cryptographic response attacks.

content.user_title: Draft Professional Peer-Review Response Letters in Word Format
content.value_statement: Organizing reviewer comments and drafting response letters for journal resubmission is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill generates a structured Word (.docx) response letter that maps each change to its exact page, paragraph, and line location for clear reviewer communication.
actual_capabilities: Merges and numbers reviewer comments from multiple reviewers with stable IDs
Classifies comments as major or minor for revision prioritization
Generates a Word (.docx) response letter with Overview for the Editor section
Maps every change to specific page, paragraph, and line locations
Includes a modification execution checklist for change verification
Quotes revised manuscript text as blockquotes for auditability
limitations: Does not edit the manuscript itself, only generates the response letter
Requires manual entry of page and line numbers when manuscript pagination changes
Cannot parse PDF reviewer comments automatically without manual input
May need adjustment for journals with non-standard response format requirements

use_cases.title: Journal Article Resubmission
use_cases.description: A researcher received 15 comments from two reviewers and needs a point-by-point response letter for journal resubmission with change tracking.

use_cases.title: Internal Manuscript Review
use_cases.description: A research team coordinates internal peer review feedback across co-authors and needs a standardized change log and execution checklist before submission.

use_cases.title: Multi-Round Revision Tracking
use_cases.description: An author manages responses across multiple revision rounds and needs to preserve comment traceability with stable IDs across iterations.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Reviewer Response
prompt_templates.prompt: I received two reviewer comments on my manuscript. Comment 1 asks for additional statistical analysis. Comment 2 requests clarification of the methodology section. Help me draft professional responses for each comment with a polite and direct tone.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with a small number of reviewer comments

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Response with Location Tracking
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to respond to 5 reviewer comments for my journal resubmission. For each comment provide: the original comment, a professional response, and the specific changes made including page, paragraph, and line numbers. Group them as major and minor comments.
prompt_templates.scenario: Structured response with precise change location marking

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Reviewer Response Letter
prompt_templates.prompt: I have feedback from 3 reviewers totaling 18 comments for a major revision. Please organize them by reviewer, add sequential numbering such as R1-1 and R2-1, classify each as major or minor, and draft point-by-point responses with change locations. Include an Overview for the Editor section at the start summarizing the major revisions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex multi-reviewer scenario with editor overview

prompt_templates.title: Complete Revision Package with Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: I am preparing a revised manuscript for resubmission after receiving 22 comments from 3 reviewers and the editor. Generate a complete response package including an Overview for the Editor, point-by-point responses with page and paragraph change tracking for every change, quoted revised manuscript excerpts in blockquote format, a modification execution checklist, and a professional polite tone throughout. The deliverable should be a structured Word document with clear section breaks for each reviewer.
prompt_templates.scenario: Full revision workflow with deliverables checklist

output_examples.input: Two reviewer comments requesting additional statistical analysis and a limitations section.
output_examples.output: The generated response letter opens with an Overview for the Editor summarizing the two key revisions. Response to R1-1 addresses the sample size concern with a detailed explanation of the power analysis conducted, citing page 5, paragraph 2 in the revised manuscript. Response to R1-2 confirms the new limitations section on page 12, paragraph 3, with a blockquote of the added text for reviewer verification. A modification checklist confirms both changes were applied.

output_examples.input: Three reviewers, 15 comments total, with one reviewer requesting major methodological changes.
output_examples.output: The letter begins with an Overview for the Editor explaining the methodological revision and its rationale. Reviewer 1 receives 6 numbered responses with the major comment addressed first. Reviewer 2 receives 5 responses covering formatting and citation updates. Reviewer 3 receives 4 minor responses. Each response includes the verbatim comment, a professional reply, and change locations marked by section heading, paragraph number, and page. An execution checklist at the end tracks all 15 changes.

best_practices: Provide exact page and paragraph numbers from your revised manuscript for accurate change location tracking.
Include the verbatim reviewer comment before each response so reviewers can follow the correspondence without cross-referencing.
Quote the revised manuscript text under Changes in Text to make every modification auditable without opening the manuscript file.
anti_patterns: Do not use table format for the response letter, as journals typically expect narrative point-by-point formatting.
Do not skip the Overview for the Editor section, as editors rely on it to quickly understand the scope of revisions.
Do not write responses before organizing comments by reviewer and numbering them sequentially, as this creates confusion for reviewers.

faq.question: What file format does this skill produce?
faq.answer: The skill produces a Word document (.docx) formatted as a professional response letter with sections for each reviewer and an overview for the editor.

faq.question: Can this skill handle multiple reviewers and revision rounds?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill assigns stable comment IDs like R1-1 and R2-1 that remain traceable across multiple revision rounds and reviewer groups.

faq.question: Do I need to provide the manuscript file?
faq.answer: No. You only need the reviewer comments and your list of manuscript changes with page, paragraph, or line references.

faq.question: What information should I prepare before using this skill?
faq.answer: Prepare the reviewer comments from all sources, your revised manuscript with change locations, and your manuscript pagination or line numbering scheme.

faq.question: Can I customize the response format for specific journal requirements?
faq.answer: The skill follows a standard three-block layout per comment: Reviewer Comment, Response, and Changes in Text. Most journals accept this format.

faq.question: Is this skill useful for non-academic review processes?
faq.answer: Yes. The structured response format works for internal document reviews, grant application feedback, and any scenario requiring point-by-point responses with change tracking.
